decks.notes: The main focus of this Boros Aggro deck is to utilize aggressive creatures such as Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls to quickly pressure the opponent. The deck also includes burn spells like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment to clear the way for attacks. Cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Seasoned Pyromancer provide additional value and card advantage. The deck also has some utility lands like Arena of Glory and Dalkovan Encampment to support the aggro strategy. Overall, the key concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with fast and efficient threats while maintaining some versatile removal options.

The focus of this Jeskai Control deck is on stalling and controlling the game through counter spells and removal, while also utilizing card advantage through card draw and cantrips. It aims to maintain board control by using a combination of removal spells like Wrath of the Skies and Galvanic Discharge, along with countermagic like Spell Snare and Counterspell. It also includes powerful late-game threats like Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury and Teferi, Hero of Dominaria to close out the game. The deck's strategy revolves around disrupting the opponent's game plan and outvaluing them in the long run.

The deck focuses on ramping up mana quickly using cards like Eldrazi Temple and Utopia Sprawl to cast powerful Eldrazi creatures such as Emrakul, the Promised End and World Breaker. It also includes control elements like Malevolent Rumble and Kozilek's Command to disrupt the opponent's strategy and protect your own creatures. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with huge threats while keeping them off balance with disruption and removal spells. Additionally, the deck includes cards like Sowing Mycospawn and Herigast, Erupting Nullkite to generate additional value and advantage over time.

This deck focuses on aggressive creature spells combined with blink effects to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy. By utilizing cards like Overlord of the Balemurk and Ephemerate to repeatedly trigger powerful enter-the-battlefield abilities, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast-paced, evasive offense. Cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push provide disruption and removal to keep the opponent on the back foot while setting up for a decisive finish with powerful creatures like Emperor of Bones and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd.

The key concept of this Modern Aggro - Affinity deck is to quickly swarm the battlefield with artifact creatures and use cards like Mox Opal and Urza's Saga to generate mana and ramp up early aggression. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of efficient creatures, mana acceleration, and disruptive spells like Metallic Rebuke. Emry, Lurker of the Loch provides card advantage and recursion for artifacts while Engineered Explosives and Pinnacle Emissary offer removal options. The inclusion of cards like Shadowspear and Tormod's Crypt helps to disrupt the opponent's strategies and maintain tempo.

The deck focuses on aggressive creature plays combined with flickering effects to generate additional value and disrupt opponents' strategies. By utilizing cards that can blink creatures in and out of play, it can protect valuable creatures, trigger powerful enter-the-battlefield effects, and create unexpected combat situations. The deck aims to quickly pressure opponents while maintaining card advantage and flexibility with its flicker effects. It combines efficient threats like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer with powerful flicker spells like Ephemerate to create a disruptive and versatile game plan.

The deck focuses on controlling the game by disrupting the opponent's mana base with land destruction spells like Cleansing Wildfire and Field of Ruin. It also uses counterspells like Wrath of the Skies and Galvanic Discharge to prevent the opponent from casting key spells. The deck contains creatures like White Orchid Phantom and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury to provide threats and close out the game. It also has removal spells like Solitude and Path to Exile to deal with opposing threats. Overall, the deck aims to maintain control of the game and outvalue the opponent.

This Dimir Control deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan through a combination of removal spells like Fatal Push and Counterspell, hand disruption with cards like Thoughtseize, and card advantage engines such as Subtlety and Cling to Dust. It also utilizes powerful threats like Kaito, Bane of Nightmares and Psychic Frog to close out the game. With a mix of control cards, efficient removal, and strong finishers, this deck aims to control the game tempo and outvalue the opponent in the long run.

The main concept of this Boros Aggro deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells. The deck focuses on playing efficient creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, while also utilizing powerful burn spells like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment to deal direct damage to the opponent. The deck also includes cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Blood Moon to further disrupt the opponent's game plan. Overall, the strategy is to apply pressure early and maintain it throughout the game to secure a swift victory.

This deck focuses on aggressive creatures that can constantly blink in and out of play to trigger powerful abilities and disrupt the opponent's board. By utilizing cards like Ephemerate and Consign to Memory, along with creatures like Phelia and Phlage, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent quickly before they can establish their own game plan. The deck also runs powerful removal spells like Prismatic Ending and Solitude to deal with threats on the opponent's side of the board, allowing the aggressive creatures to continue pushing through for game-winning damage.

The deck is focused on generating mana quickly and efficiently to cast powerful spells that deal damage and draw cards. It uses a combination of rituals, medallions, and card draw effects to build towards a game-winning turn with cards like Ral, Monsoon Mage and Grapeshot. The deck aims to chain together spells and make use of storm mechanics to overwhelm the opponent with a flurry of spells in a single turn. The addition of Wish allows for versatile sideboard options to adapt to different matchups.

The main strategy of this deck is to quickly and aggressively deal damage to the opponent by utilizing spells and creatures that can do so efficiently. By combining cheap spells like Lava Dart, Lightning Bolt, and Preordain with creatures like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Monastery Swiftspear, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and end the game quickly. The deck also includes cards like Scalding Tarn and Mishra's Bauble to help with card selection and consistency, ensuring that the aggressive game plan can be executed effectively.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card draw. It utilizes powerful planeswalkers like Narset, Parter of Veils and Teferi, Time Raveler to disrupt the opponent's strategy and generate card advantage. The deck also includes efficient removal spells like Prismatic Ending and Solitude to deal with threats on the board. By managing resources carefully and disrupting the opponent's game plan, the deck aims to outlast the opponent and secure victory through incremental advantage and card advantage.

This deck revolves around using blink effects to repeatedly abuse powerful enter-the-battlefield abilities of creatures like Overlord of the Balemurk and Witch Enchanter. By constantly flickering these creatures with cards like Ephemerate and Flickerwisp, the deck gains repeated value and disruption. The deck also includes control elements like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Teferi, Time Raveler provides additional disruption and protection, while Emperor of Bones and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd offer strong aggro threats to close out the game quickly.

The deck focuses on aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, supported by burn spells like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment to quickly take down the opponent's life total. It also includes utility cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Seasoned Pyromancer for additional value and protection. The mana base is built around fetch lands like Arid Mesa and Flooded Strand to ensure consistent mana access, with cards like Sacred Foundry and Marsh Flats providing the necessary colors for the deck's spells. The inclusion of cards like Blood Moon and Path to Exile offer disruption and removal options to deal with threats.

The deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan through counterspells, removal spells, and hand disruption. It utilizes cards like Counterspell, Fatal Push, and Undercity Sewers to control the board, while also using cards like Consult the Star Charts and Wan Shi Tong, Librarian to draw cards and maintain card advantage. With a mix of control and tempo cards, the deck aims to outvalue the opponent and win the game through attrition.
